spans.text: — then see the breathless form , Which thy misdeeds have brought , Hear
the breathless form , Which thy misdeeds have brought
, monsters , why did you destroy That life ye’re here to give
Bute himself be forc’d to see The deadly waste
Let Bute himself be forc’d to see The deadly waste
feel with tortur’d heart The stroke he lately gave , For Britons will lament the deed
one night or day Till full revenge she got
much If Grenville would firmly stand
) Cut off my bloom so soon , And let my lasting night come on Amid its perfect noon
my lasting night come on Amid its perfect noon
What right you had to act And judge against the well known law Of freedom and of fact
the well known law Of freedom and of fact
a Patriot part , And yet that vow forsake
to win my heart , Yet cause that Heart to break
Yet truly prove those gilded words To be of none effect
those gilded words To be of none effect
will — what not? — to please that will Been forc’d to undergo
are the few who lov’d me dear ?
perfidious why these limbs Were suffer’d thus to spoil
those pangs ye’re soon to feel In that tormenting bed
Your wicked deeds have wrought the change , A lion to a mouse
